title: Pass Record
description: Lightweight evidence bridge written at the end of each game pass. Captures what was proved, what was broken and fixed, what remains open, and what Pass N+1 must address first. Not a new pipeline stage — written during proof-case review.

pass_name.description: Unique slug for this pass. Recommended: '<game>-pass-<n>' e.g. 'bakery-rush-pass-1'.

timestamp.description: ISO-8601 timestamp when this record was written.

what_this_pass_proved.description: Specific things the playable preview demonstrated to be true. Each item should be a falsifiable claim, not a wish. e.g. 'The core loop is learnable without instruction overlay.'

open_questions.description: Questions this pass raised but did not answer. Carried forward to Pass N+1.

description.description: What was broken and what was done to fix it.

impact.description: Which quality dimension the fix addressed. teaching=learning loop; correctness=logic/state; honesty=visible but nonfunctional element; polish=language/presentation.

bugs_fixed.description: Bugs caught and fixed during this pass. Evidence that the proof case was used, not just built.

known_limits.description: Things intentionally left incomplete or deferred. Distinguishes deliberate scope from unfinished work.

auditor_results.description: Optional. Output from difficulty_ramp_auditor.py if it was run against this pass.

next_pass_first_obligation.description: Single sentence stating what Pass N+1 must address before anything else. Not a wish list — one specific claim or fix. e.g. 'Prove the selection constraint is learnable by a player who has never played a subset-sum game.'
